page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B processor operating at 2.9 GHz, configured with 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs at 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or hardware GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance asymmetry between single-core and multi-core operations. The instance achieves average performance in single-core metrics, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1858.29 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1559.0. However, multi-core and memory-intensive tasks show significant limitations. The multi-core stress-ng score of 1932.77 and Geekbench multi-core score of 1813.0 are categorized as poor, reflecting the constraints of the 2-vCPU configuration. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large-block read and write speeds recorded at 14.89 GB/sec and 13.52 GB/sec respectively, both falling into the bottom 10% of tested hardware.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c7se.large offers a dedicated CPU allocation at an entry-level scale, making it suitable for workloads that require consistent CPU performance without the virtualization overhead of shared-core instances. However, the lack of local storage and the low memory-to-vCPU ratio represent significant tradeoffs, limiting its capability for data-intensive applications. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, simple text processing, or utility services that do not demand high memory throughput or parallel processing capabilities.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.large is an entry-level, compute-oriented virtual machine instance built on the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz with 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 4.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks reveal average single-core capabilities, such as a Geekbench single-core score of 1559.0, but poor multi-core and memory bandwidth results due to its low resource scale. This hardware profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for dedicated single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and lightweight web serving, though it is poorly suited for memory-intensive databases or parallelized workloads.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.large is a 2-vCPU, 4 GB RAM virtual machine featuring a dedicated 2.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for lightweight compute tasks but limited by low memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.large is a KVM-based instance with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU for basic compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core compute instance designed for lightweight single-threaded processing and entry-level utility workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz
2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM with a 1:2 compute-optimized ratio
Average single-core performance suitable for basic text and utility processing
No local storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps
Poor multi-core and memory bandwidth performance under heavy parallel loads
